# QaMRpp: Feature-Rich, Header-Only Implementation of Lua, with a CLI

QaMRpp is the slopware I made that convinced me Agentic AI does not take the satisfaction of building software away, rather, it boosts it up. I'd like you to direct you to Peter Naur (the 'N' in BNF/EBNF) 1985 paper "Programming as Theory-Building", which is one Google search away. In this paper, Naur argues that programmers are not artisans whose hammer is the keyboard and their nail is the stream of characters they write unto their terminals, rather, a *good* programmer is a "theory builder", like a mathematician. Except, a "good" programmer makes dozens of theories a day (especially if he is supplied with caffy, addy, ritty, or lizzy --- mine is lotsa ritty, complemented with the once in a while lizzy).

Retrace the Git history of this repository. 'Tis the fist slopware I made. Skeptical, angry, and confused. And now I have an implementation of Lua 5.4 that does not only yield a CLI, with syntax highlighting, using one of my later slopware [PikoRL](https://github.com/Chubek/PikoRL) but I also have a Lua like none other: a header-only library, with the ability to make plugins for, and libraries too. In fact, it implements the entire Lua standard library in `stdlib`, and some extra C++-specific supersets in `stdlib++`.
